message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  A time-series model was developed for
                  <jats:italic>Number of Total Infected Cases in India</jats:italic>
                  , using data from
                  <jats:italic>Mar 3 to May 7, 2020</jats:italic>
                  . Two models developed in the early phases were discarded when they lost statistical validity, The third, current, model is a 3
                  <jats:sup>rd</jats:sup>
                  -degree polynomial that has remained
                  <jats:italic>
                    stable over the last 30 days (since Apr 8), with R
                    <jats:sup>2</jats:sup>
                    > 0.998 consistently
                  </jats:italic>
                  . This model is used to forecast Total Covid cases, after cautionary discussion of triggers that would invalidate the model. The purpose of all forecasts in the study is to provide
                  <jats:italic>a comparator</jats:italic>
                  to evaluate policy initiatives to control the pandemic - the forecasts are not objectives by themselves. Actual observations less than forecasts mean successful policy interventions. Figures of
                  <jats:italic>Doubling Time</jats:italic>
                  , Fatality Rate and Recovery Rate used by authorities are questioned. Elongation of doubling rates is inherent in the model, and worthy of mention only when the time actually exceeds what the model predicts. The popular
                  <jats:italic>Fatality Rate</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>Recovery Rate</jats:italic>
                  metrics are shown to be illogical. The study defines two terms
                  <jats:italic>Ongoing Fatality Rate (OFR)</jats:italic>
                  , and
                  <jats:italic>Ongoing Recovery Rate (ORR)</jats:italic>
                  and determines these currently to be
                  <jats:italic>~9% and ~76%</jats:italic>
                  respectively in India. Over time, OFR will decline to the eventual Case Fatality Rate (CFR), while ORR will eventually climb to (1-CFR). There is no statistical basis to assume eventual Indian CFR, and China’s 5.5% CFR is used as a proxy.
                  <jats:italic>Using these metrics, the current model forecasts by May-end, >150000 Total Infected, ~5000 Deaths and >85000 Active Cases</jats:italic>
                  . There is no
                  <jats:italic>pull-back</jats:italic>
                  evident in the current model in the foreseeable future, and cases continue to rise at progressively slower rates. Subject to usual caveats, the model is used to forecast till Sep 15.
                  <jats:italic>The study argues that Indian hospital infrastructure is reasonably ready to handle Active Cases as predicted for Sept 15</jats:italic>
                  – in that sense, the curve is “flat enough”.
                  <jats:italic>However, the curve is NOT flat enough with respect to fatalities - nearly 100000 by Sept 15</jats:italic>
                  . Setting an arbitrary limit that Total Deaths must be within 50,000 by Sept 15, the study retrofits a model that shows what the desired growth of Covid19 cases should be.
                  <jats:italic>It is seen that overall doubling time of 38 days is required in period June 1 to Sep 15, if deaths are to remain below 50000</jats:italic>
                  .
                </jats:p>
title: MODELING COVID19 IN INDIA (Mar 3-May 7, 2020): HOW FLAT IS FLAT, AND OTHER HARD FACTS

reference.unstructured: https://www.ndtv.com/coronavirus/india-covid-19-tracker quoting Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, India (https://www.mohfw.gov.in/) for daily data on Covid19 statistics in India, updated 8am daily.
Lord, Debbie , April 8 2020 (updated), Coronavirus: How Covid-19 Progresses; a day-by-day breakdown of symptoms; www.boston25news.com
Harsh Vardhan , Union Minister for Heath & Family Welfare, My 9, 2020, Prepared for Worst in Fight Against Coronavirus, Speech to Health Officers North-East, reported by www.ndtv.com
www.indiatoday.in, April 22, 2020, News Report: India Ready with 4 lakh beds in 400 districts to tackle Covid 19.
